title: Mobile Sensor-based Biometrics using Common Daily Activities

method.input_representation: 43 descriptive high-level features (Average, Standard Deviation, Average Absolute Difference, Time Between Peaks, Average Resultant Acceleration, Binned Distribution)
method.training_strategy: Stratified 10-fold cross-validation for identification; Binary classification with 1:3 user-to-imposter under-sampling for authentication.

evaluation.context: Best result achieved on 'Pasta' eating activity using the 'Accel' sensor combination (phone and watch accelerometers) with Random Forest and voting.

evaluation_protocol: Majority voting using 5 examples (50 seconds of data) to improve identification and authentication results.

reported_limitations: Activities involving eating or drinking had the worst performance overall
Writing and typing are less practical due to requirement of external equipment
Exercise related activities require open space and equipment

usability.usability_issues: Requirement for specific equipment (keyboard, pen, food) for several of the tested activities.

key_contribution: Comprehensive evaluation of 18 daily activities for biometric efficacy
Comparison of 9 sensor combinations across smartphone and smartwatch
main_strength: Large subject pool (51) compared to typical motion biometric studies
Demonstration that activity labels are not strictly necessary for high identification accuracy
main_weakness: Data collected in a single session (no temporal robustness)
insights.notes_for_review: The paper establishes a strong baseline for multiple activities but highlights the need for longitudinal data to verify stability over time.
